title: throw special error if rejected promises are incorrectly instrumented
body:
## Summary

When a rejected Promise has its rejection reason set in the wrong field during userspace instrumentation, React throws `undefined`. This makes the root cause extremely difficult to diagnose because the thrown value carries no useful message or stack.

## Proposed fix

A React Core Team member is proposing a targeted fix: when we detect that the rejection reason is `undefined` but the promise has a non-undefined value on the incorrect field, we throw a generic `Error` with a useful message and callstack instead. This would apply in both dev and prod.

## Reproduction

See the linked PR for a minimal reproduction script.
summary: React throws `undefined` when a rejected Promise's rejection reason is stored in the wrong field during userspace instrumentation, making root-cause diagnosis nearly impossible. A Core Team member has proposed a targeted fix with a minimal reproduction attached.

title: [compiler Bug]: AST contract violation: babel-plugin-react-compiler writes `Symbol()` into Node.loc, breaks downstream serialization (jest-worker / v8.serialize / IPC)
body:
## React version
17.0.2 and 18.3.1

## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable babel-plugin-react-compiler in a Jest project using jest-worker.
2. Run any test that imports a component compiled by the plugin.
3. Observe `DataCloneError: Symbol() could not be cloned` thrown by v8.serialize during IPC.

## Expected behaviour

The plugin should leave `Node.loc` as `null` (per the Babel spec) when no source location is available.

## Actual behaviour

The compiler writes a `Symbol()` sentinel into `Node.loc`, violating the `babel` `Node.loc: sourceLocation | null` contract. `v8.serialize` cannot clone Symbols, so any downstream serialization (jest-worker IPC, child_process.send) throws.

## Minimal reproduction

https://github.com/example/repro-symbol-loc
summary: The babel-plugin-react-compiler writes a `Symbol()` into `Node.loc` instead of `null`, breaking `v8.serialize` in jest-worker and other IPC channels with a `DataCloneError`. Reproducible on React 17.0.2 and 18.3.1 with a minimal reproduction linked.

title: [eslint-plugin-react-hooks] include cycle-entry segment in cyclic set
body:
## Bug Report

The `RulesOfHooks` lint rule does not flag hooks called in the test expression of `while` or `for` loops.

## Reproduction

```js
function Component() {
  while (useCondition()) { // not flagged — should be an error
    // ...
  }
}
```

## Root cause

The cycle-entry segment is excluded from the `cyclic` set due to an off-by-one in the `indexOf` slice. The slice starts one position too late, so the first segment of the cycle is never added to the set.

## Fix

A PR with new test cases (failing on `main`, passing with the fix) is attached.
summary: The `RulesOfHooks` ESLint rule fails to flag hooks called in `while`/`for` loop test expressions due to an off-by-one error that excludes the cycle-entry segment from the cyclic set. A fix PR with failing-then-passing test cases is already attached.

title: Add regression tests for strictMode transition unmount cleanup
body:
This PR adds test-only coverage for `useEffect` cleanup behavior during StrictMode concurrent transition unmounts. There are no changes to production runtime logic.

Adds:
- 3 new test cases in ReactStrictMode-test.js
- No changes outside of test files
summary: This submission adds test-only coverage for `useEffect` cleanup during StrictMode concurrent transition unmounts with no production code changes. It is a maintenance contribution rather than a bug or feature request.

title: fix false hydration mismatch when client renders portal that returned null on server
body:
## Problem

When a component returns `null` on the server but renders a `ReactDOM.createPortal()` on the client, React incorrectly emits a hydration mismatch error.

## Root cause

The portal's hydration state is not isolated from the parent container's. When the portal is encountered during client hydration, the reconciler checks the parent's hydration cursor and finds unexpected DOM nodes.

## Fix

Proposes adding `prepareToHydrateHostPortal` and `popHydrationStateAfterPortal` helpers (mirroring the existing host instance helpers). New passing tests included.

## React version
18.3.1

## Reproduction

https://codesandbox.io/s/portal-hydration-mismatch
summary: React emits a false hydration mismatch error when a portal component returns `null` on the server but renders on the client, because portal hydration state is not isolated from the parent container. A fix proposing new hydration helpers is included alongside a CodeSandbox reproduction on React 18.3.1.

title: Bug: typo in error message
body:
## Description

The error message for hoisted `<style>` tags with spaces in the `href` prop misspells "occurred" as "ocurred".

## Location

`packages/react-dom/src/client/ReactDOMHostConfig.js`

## Fix

A linked PR (#36319) already corrects the spelling.
summary: A typo in an error message spells "occurred" as "ocurred" in `ReactDOMHostConfig.js`. A fix PR is already open, making this issue redundant rather than independently actionable.

title: Bug: useOptimistic fails to revert when independent async actions overlap due to global scope entanglement
body:
## Summary

`useOptimistic` optimistic state fails to revert to the base state after its action completes when another unrelated async action is still pending in a different component.

## Root cause

The revert mechanism in `ReactFiberAsyncAction.js` is gated on a global per-root pending action count. A slow async action in `ComponentA` increments this counter, preventing `ComponentB`'s optimistic state from reverting even after `ComponentB`'s own action has finished.

## Reproduction

https://codesandbox.io/s/useoptimistic-global-entanglement

## React version

19.0.0
summary: `useOptimistic` state in one component fails to revert after its action completes when an unrelated async action in another component is still pending, due to a global per-root action counter in `ReactFiberAsyncAction.js`. Reproducible on React 19.0.0 with a CodeSandbox demonstrating the entanglement.

title: [verified] fix(reconciler): recheck store snapshot after subscribe
body:
## Problem

When `useSyncExternalStore` is used with Suspense and StrictMode, the remount path skips the post-subscribe snapshot re-check. This can cause the hook to miss a store mutation that occurs during `subscribe(handleStoreChange)`.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Use `useSyncExternalStore` inside a Suspense boundary with StrictMode enabled.
2. Have the store update synchronously inside the `subscribe` callback.
3. Observe that the UI reflects stale data until the next render.

## Fix

Re-check the snapshot immediately after `subscribe(handleStoreChange)` returns.
Related: #35550

## React version
18.3.1
summary: `useSyncExternalStore` can display stale data when used with Suspense and StrictMode, because the remount path skips the post-subscribe snapshot re-check. The fix requires re-checking the snapshot immediately after `subscribe` returns; reproducible on React 18.3.1 with clear steps.
